/// A simple in-memory key-value cache with bounded capacity.
pub struct CacheLayer {
    entries: std::collections::HashMap<String, Vec<u8>>,
    capacity: usize,
}

impl CacheLayer {
    /// Creates a new cache with the given capacity.
    ///
    /// Uses a `HashMap` with pre-allocated space. Eviction is not yet
    /// implemented; callers should bound the entry count externally.
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            entries: std::collections::HashMap::with_capacity(capacity),
            capacity,
        }
    }

    /// Inserts a value into the cache, returning the previous value if present.
    pub fn insert(&mut self, key: String, value: Vec<u8>) -> Option<Vec<u8>> {
        self.entries.insert(key, value)
    }

    /// Retrieves a cached value by key.
    pub fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<&[u8]> {
        self.entries.get(key).map(|v| v.as_slice())
    }
}
